proposed_answer: Hands contain many small bones, nerves, and muscles needed for precise function, so a finger injury can have lasting effects.
proposed_question: Why does Rope365 urge extra safety caution when tying fingers?
reason: This preserves the anatomical reason and lasting-impact warning without operationalizing the page's risky finger restraints.
support_evidence: Hands are a fascinating part of the body with their many bones, nerves, muscles, and their ability to feel and manipulate objects with precision. There is also a very powerful psychological aspect of controlling the movement of hands and fingers. This complexity comes at the cost of fragility, we have to be mindful when tying the fingers as injuring them can have lasting effects on someone’s life.

proposed_answer: They change how the rope feels and how the ankles behave, add some foot protection, and can provide heel attachment points.
proposed_question: How can shoes change a foot tie according to Rope365?
reason: This combines the source's three stated effects without treating shoes as sufficient protection.
support_evidence: Day 169: Shoes and Heels – Tying with shoes on changes how the rope feels and ankles will behave. It adds some protection to the feet and heels make an interesting attachment point. Bonus: how is tying with socks different from a bare foot.

proposed_answer: Apply the shape or pattern to different body parts or structures, such as moving a familiar box-tie pattern to the lower body.
proposed_question: How does Rope365 suggest using an existing pattern to generate a new tie?
reason: This turns the day prompt into a transferable creative method while retaining its concrete example.
support_evidence: | Day 255: Pattern – Pick a shape, a pattern, and apply the technique to different body parts and structures. Make diamonds everywhere on the body. How many types of ladder can you make? Can you tie your favorite box tie pattern on your lower body? |
